description: Azure-specific context rules and naming conventions

required_policies: Require managed identity for App Services

on_startup.message: Azure context loaded. Found {recent_azure.count} stored configurations.

cost_metrics.per_customer_avg: average monthly cost per private customer

sftp_storage_workflows.description: Complete workflows for creating Azure Storage Accounts with SFTP access for temporary customer data exchange

notes: Storage account name max 24 characters, lowercase alphanumeric only
Customer name should be max 9 characters for proper naming
tmp prefix clearly marks temporary resources for lifecycle management

critical_requirements.hierarchical_namespace: MUST be enabled for SFTP support - this cannot be changed after creation
critical_requirements.sftp_enable: Must be explicitly enabled on storage account
critical_requirements.authentication: Password-based authentication is simpler than SSH keys for customer setup

step_1_resource_group.example: az group create --name rg-acmecorp-storage --location southcentralus --subscription xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
step_1_resource_group.validation: Check if resource group appears in az group list
step_1_resource_group.common_issue: Resource group may appear created but not be immediately queryable - retry if needed

step_2_storage_account.command: az storage account create --name tmp[CUSTOMER]sftpdata --resource-group rg-[CUSTOMER]-storage --location [LOCATION] --sku Standard_LRS --kind StorageV2 --enable-hierarchical-namespace true --enable-sftp true --subscription [SUBSCRIPTION]
step_2_storage_account.example: az storage account create --name tmpacmecorpsftpdata --resource-group rg-acmecorp-storage --location southcentralus --sku Standard_LRS --kind StorageV2 --enable-hierarchical-namespace true --enable-sftp true --subscription xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

critical_flags.enable-hierarchical-namespace: true - REQUIRED for SFTP, cannot be changed later

step_2_storage_account.validation: Check with: az storage account show --name tmp[CUSTOMER]sftpdata
step_2_storage_account.common_issue: Name must be globally unique and use only lowercase letters and numbers

step_3_container.command: az storage container create --name data --account-name tmp[CUSTOMER]sftpdata --resource-group rg-[CUSTOMER]-storage --subscription [SUBSCRIPTION]
step_3_container.example: az storage container create --name data --account-name tmpacmecorpsftpdata --resource-group rg-acmecorp-storage --subscription xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
step_3_container.notes: Container name will be the root directory for SFTP access

step_4_local_user.command: az storage account local-user create --account-name tmp[CUSTOMER]sftpdata --resource-group rg-[CUSTOMER]-storage --name [CUSTOMER]user --permission-scope permissions=rwdlc service=blob resource-name=data --home-directory data --subscription [SUBSCRIPTION]
step_4_local_user.example: az storage account local-user create --account-name tmpacmecorpsftpdata --resource-group rg-acmecorp-storage --name acmecorpuser --permission-scope permissions=rwdlc service=blob resource-name=data --home-directory data --subscription xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

permissions_explained.r: read - Read files and list directories
permissions_explained.c: create - Create new files and directories

step_4_local_user.home_directory: Set to container name to make it the root access point
notes: Local user name should match customer name for clarity
Full permissions (rwdlc) give complete control over the data container

step_5_generate_password.command: az storage account local-user regenerate-password --account-name tmp[CUSTOMER]sftpdata --resource-group rg-[CUSTOMER]-storage --name [CUSTOMER]user --subscription [SUBSCRIPTION]
step_5_generate_password.example: az storage account local-user regenerate-password --account-name tmpacmecorpsftpdata --resource-group rg-acmecorp-storage --name acmecorpuser --subscription xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
step_5_generate_password.security_note: Password is only shown once - ensure it's securely stored and transmitted

step_6_apply_tags.command: az group update --name rg-[CUSTOMER]-storage --tags department=hosting customer=[customer] project=sftp-storage duration=temporary environment=temporary created-date=[YYYY-MM-DD] owner=hosting-team review-date=[YYYY-MM-DD]
step_6_apply_tags.example: az group update --name rg-acmecorp-storage --tags department=hosting customer=acmecorp project=sftp-storage duration=temporary environment=temporary created-date=2025-09-24 owner=hosting-team review-date=2025-10-24
step_6_apply_tags.purpose: Lifecycle management and cost tracking for temporary resources

connection_information_template.format: Provide to customer in clear, copy-paste friendly format
customer_instructions: Use any standard SFTP client (FileZilla, WinSCP, Cyberduck)
Home directory /data is already set as default root

temporary_markers.naming: tmp prefix in storage account name
temporary_markers.documentation: Always emphasize temporary nature in communications

review_process.timeline: Set review date typically 1 month after creation
action_items: Verify if still needed with customer
Plan deletion if no longer required
Extend if additional time needed (with new review date)

deletion_checklist: Confirm with customer that data is no longer needed
Export/backup any critical data if required
Delete storage account (includes all containers and data)
Delete resource group if no other resources

issue_1.problem: Storage account creation fails with 'name already taken'
issue_1.solution: Storage account names must be globally unique across all Azure. Try adding region code or date suffix.

issue_2.problem: Cannot enable SFTP - hierarchical namespace not enabled
issue_2.solution: HNS must be enabled at storage account creation time. Cannot be added later. Recreate storage account with --enable-hierarchical-namespace true

issue_3.solution: Verify username format includes storage account prefix: [account].[user]@[account].blob.core.windows.net

issue_4.problem: User has no permissions despite configuration
issue_4.solution: Check permission-scope parameter includes all required permissions (rwdlc) and correct resource-name (container name)

issue_5.problem: Resource group not found immediately after creation
issue_5.solution: Azure propagation delay - wait 30 seconds and retry, or verify in Azure Portal

security_considerations.authentication: Password-based is simpler for temporary setups; SSH keys for long-term
security_considerations.network_access: Default allows public access - consider adding firewall rules for production
security_considerations.data_encryption: Encrypted at rest by default, SFTP uses TLS in transit
security_considerations.monitoring: Enable diagnostic logs for audit trail of file access

cost_implications.data_storage: Based on amount of data stored (Standard_LRS is most economical)
cost_implications.transactions: SFTP operations incur standard blob transaction costs
cost_implications.egress: Data transfer out of Azure may incur bandwidth charges
cost_implications.recommendation: Monitor costs through Azure Cost Management for temporary setups
